Survey: 'No problem'

November 1995
Government & Politics

Carl Malamud of Internet Multicasting Service dismisses the SEC's survey on EDGAR access, emphasizing that the actual online database implementation matters more than the agency's information request.

One of the key proponents for free access to EDGAR said he is not worried about the SEC's survey.

"Actions speak louder than words," said Carl Malamud of the non-profit Internet Multicasting Service. "I think the on-line database is of more significance than an RFI (request information)."
